decisions.briefing: Market is neutral today with a slight divergence between NASDAQ (-0.4%) and S&P 500 (+0.3%), suggesting mild rotation out of tech into broader market names. Cash sits at $477 — just below the $500 threshold to trigger new buys — so no new positions are warranted today. Current holdings remain intact; the portfolio's 31.6% gain provides a solid cushion and there's no reason to force action on a mixed tape.

market_education.summary_en: U.S. equities posted a mixed session on June 25, 2026, with the S&P 500 edging up 0.3% while the NASDAQ slipped 0.4%, reflecting a modest rotation out of growth and technology shares into broader, value-oriented sectors. This divergence is common when investors reassess risk appetite mid-week, trimming high-multiple tech positions while adding to industrials and financials. No major macro catalysts were cited, keeping overall volatility subdued.
market_education.summary_zh: 2026年6月25日美股表現分化，標普500指數微升0.3%，納斯達克指數則小跌0.4%，顯示市場資金從科技成長股輪動至價值類板塊。此類走勢在週中常見，投資者趁機調整高估值科技持倉，轉而增持工業及金融類股。整體市場欠缺重大宏觀催化劑，波動率維持溫和。

daily_lesson.explanation_en: Sector rotation is when investors move money out of one area of the market (like technology) and into another (like industrials or financials), often in response to changing economic expectations or risk appetite. Today's split — NASDAQ down while the S&P 500 rose — is a classic signal of this happening. Understanding rotation helps you avoid holding the wrong sectors at the wrong time.
daily_lesson.explanation_zh: 板塊輪動是指投資者將資金從某一市場板塊（如科技股）移出，轉移至另一板塊（如工業或金融股），通常反映對經濟前景或風險偏好的轉變。今日納斯達克下跌而標普500上升，正是輪動訊號的典型表現。掌握輪動規律有助於避免在錯誤時機持有錯誤板塊，從而提升組合的風險調整後回報。
